intro: Selbyana crinita, also known as the "hairy polypore," is a type of fungi that belongs to the polyporoid family of mushroom-like organisms. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Selbyana crinita include triterpenoids, polysaccharides, and phenolic compounds, which have been found to possess antimicrobial, antiviral, and antitumor activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Selbyana crinita include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions and as a natural remedy for immune system support.
